name: Typography
description: The marketing typeface pairing — Montserrat for display/headline use and Roboto for body copy — plus case, tracking/leading, readability, type-scale, and editorial (widow/orphan) rules.
keyRule: Montserrat is a display face only. It appears in headline/font-pairing examples but never as the body typeface — Roboto carries all body copy. Don't use Montserrat for long-form text.

pages.name: Font pairing examples
pages.description: Shows Montserrat headline + Roboto body together in a realistic layout, establishing the default editorial pattern.

pages.name: Roboto type cases
pages.description: Approved case treatments for Roboto (sentence case for body copy; specific rules for labels/eyebrows shown alongside).

pages.name: Roboto readability
pages.description: Guidance on line length, line-height, and paragraph spacing for legible body copy.

pages.name: Montserrat type cases
pages.description: Approved case treatments for Montserrat headlines (appears to favour a bolder, more condensed treatment for impact headlines).

pages.name: Montserrat readability
pages.description: Sizing/weight guidance specific to Montserrat at headline scale.

pages.name: Type case usage
pages.description: Cross-typeface rules for when to use uppercase/sentence case/title case across both faces.

pages.name: Tracking and leading
pages.description: Letter-spacing and line-height values per size step (exact values: TBD — verify against Figma).

pages.name: Widows, orphans and suspended lines
pages.description: Editorial typesetting rule — instructs against leaving a single short word/line stranded at the top or bottom of a text block, shown with a 'bad' and 'fixed' example side by side. An unusually print-editorial level of care for a digital brand guideline.

pages.name: Example / Example — fixed
pages.description: Full worked examples applying the above rules to a realistic paragraph of body copy, including a 'before' with a bad line break and an 'after' fix.

doThis: Pair Montserrat headlines with Roboto body copy — don't mix headline faces.
Follow the case rules per typeface rather than defaulting to uppercase for emphasis.
Check line breaks for widows/orphans before shipping any long-form copy.
dontDoThis: Don't use Montserrat for body copy or long-form text.
Don't leave a single stranded word/short line at a paragraph break.
Don't invent tracking/leading values outside what's specified.
relationToProductTokens: Product UI font stack (semantic.typography.fontFamily → primitive.fontFamily.muiDefault = "Roboto", "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif) has no Montserrat anywhere. Roboto overlaps as the shared base typeface, but the product never uses a separate display face the way marketing uses Montserrat. Read as intentional: Montserrat is reserved for marketing headlines, not product UI — worth stating explicitly in tokens.json's fontFamily notes so it doesn't look like an oversight later.
openQuestions: Pull exact tracking/leading and type-scale (px/rem) values from Figma directly.
Confirm whether Montserrat is used anywhere in-product (e.g. marketing-facing pages served from the same app) — if so, it needs a token; if not, it stays brand-only.
